config.title: Sachsenhausen from Berlin - FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers for tourists planning a respectful memorial visit from central Berlin.

items.q: How do you get to Sachsenhausen from Berlin?
items.a: Take the S1, RE5, RB12 or RB32 to Oranienburg, then either walk about 20 minutes or use bus 804 or 821 to the Gedenkstaette stop. From central Berlin, the memorial says Berlin ABC is the normal fare zone.

items.q: Is Sachsenhausen Memorial free to visit?
items.a: Yes. Admission to the Sachsenhausen Memorial and its museums is free. Audio guides and guided tours cost extra.

items.q: How long do you need for Sachsenhausen from Berlin?
items.a: For most tourists, plan about 4.5 to 6 hours door-to-door from central Berlin. The site can technically be visited faster, but a rushed memorial visit is the wrong choice.

items.q: Can I visit Sachsenhausen without a guided tour?
items.a: Yes. You can visit independently and rent an audio guide at the Visitor Information Centre. A guided tour is helpful if you want structure, context and a slower route through the site.

items.q: What are the opening hours for Sachsenhausen Memorial?
items.a: The Visitor Information Centre is open daily from 8:30 to 17:00. Outdoor areas and exhibitions currently open 8:30 to 18:00 in summer and 8:30 to 16:30 in winter. Check the official memorial page before leaving.
The memorial recommends public tours for visitors aged 14 and older. Families with younger children should think carefully about maturity, energy and the emotional weight of the visit.

items.q: Should I visit Sachsenhausen or Topography of Terror?
items.a: Choose Sachsenhausen if you can give a dedicated half day to a former concentration camp memorial outside Berlin. Choose Topography of Terror if you need a central, free, shorter documentation site near Potsdamer Platz.

items.q: Can I combine Sachsenhausen with BerlinWalk?
items.a: Yes, but not as a rushed same-afternoon add-on. BerlinWalk lasts about 2 hours in the historic centre, so Sachsenhausen works better on a separate morning or as the main plan of another day.
